findings.problem: After June's authorship and money exploitation are already established, the chapter keeps converting live danger into explicit motive-summary. The confrontation inventories jealousy, class resentment, possession, and desire so completely that it sheds some of the negative space and residue an award-level scene needs.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the explanatory blocks beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:283, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:379, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:471 so at least one core motive is carried by deflection, loaded omission, or behavior rather than a clean self-diagnostic paragraph. Keep jealousy, opportunism, and the thrill of precision legible, but do not let June explain all three at full essay clarity.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised passages beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:283, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:379, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:471, each block must either reveal a genuinely new motive or materially change the power balance; if all three passages still function mainly as direct explanation of June's grievance architecture, fail.

findings.problem: The scene reaches its sharpest irreversible admission when June answers 'Sometimes' to sexual gratification, but the chapter then runs two more confession loops before the debt and severance terms land. That post-peak stretch restates adjacent injuries rather than escalating through new consequence, so the confrontation plateaus instead of tightening.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or restructure the post-admission material beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:369 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:461. After the beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:353, any remaining exchange should trigger a new consequence, interruption, or concrete choice, not another paraphrase of why June did it.
findings.acceptance_test: After the revised equivalent of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:353, every subsequent exchange through the severance terms at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:509 must either alter Lena's next move or introduce a cost that was not already on the table; if a later confession block can be removed without changing Lena's debt/boundary terms, fail.

findings.problem: June's voice collapses into repeated monosyllabic 'Yeah' admissions across multiple high-pressure exchanges. That creates transcript cadence, violates the style bible's cap on consecutive low-information replies, and flattens a character who is supposed to become quieter and more exact under pressure, not generically blank.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise June's replies at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:339, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:343, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:347, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:409, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:457, and the admission run beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:487 so no more than one consecutive reply is low-information. Keep the admissions incriminating, but let June answer in her practical, controlling idiolect by adding a new fact, sharper phrasing, or a real attempt to frame the exchange.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised exchanges beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:337 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:483, June may use at most one consecutive monosyllabic acknowledgment; every later reply must add a new detail, motive, or leverage shift that Lena did not already have.

findings.problem: The narration repeatedly falls into clipped, subject-dropped fragments such as 'Not long,' 'Not complete,' and 'Not tears.' Used this often, the move becomes a visible tic and directly violates the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration. In a confrontation chapter that depends on sentence control, that mannerism is costly.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the clipped narration around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:139, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:143, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:251, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:355, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:395 into grammatically complete sentences that preserve pressure without relying on omitted subjects or verbs.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised narration at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:139, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:143, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:251, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:355, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:395, the same beats must be rendered in complete sentences; if any cited narrative sentence still depends on subject-dropping or article-stripping for punch, fail.

summary: Chapter 16 preserves continuity, canonical past tense, and much of the style bible's raw specificity, and Lena and June are mostly recognizable in voice. It still fails this review pass because the confrontation over-explains June after its strongest turn, the post-peak structure plateaus before severance, June's late-scene dialogue collapses into repeated low-information admissions, and the narration repeatedly uses constitution-barred clipped fragments.
